title: didiplis diandra f. aquatica
intro: Didiplis diandra f. aquatica, also known as water-shoulder, is a semi-aquatic plant species belonging to the family Lythraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. Its health benefits include treating various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues like coughs and colds. The plant can be prepared as a herbal infusion or decoction, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine as a poultice or topical application to treat various ailments.
